growth_records.strain: Enrichment culture B (tidal wetland soil-derived, CO2/H2-grown, Caproicibacter sp.-dominated with minor C. thailandense)
growth_records.medium: Modified PETC-MES medium + 1.5 g/L yeast extract, CO2/H2 gas feed
growth_records.cite: Rodriguez et al. Abundant and active acetogens enhance the carbon dioxide sink of Blue Carbon ecosystems. Microbiome (2025). DOI:10.1186/s40168-025-02209-4. [PMC12529827]
growth_records.snippet: Both cultures started consuming CO2 and H2 after ~24 h from inoculation in parallel to exponential growth. During this growth phase, cultures A and B exhibited growth rates of 0.039 and 0.029 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Specific growth rate (mu_max = 0.029 h-1) directly reported for enrichment culture B, calculated by the authors from 3 exponential-phase OD600 data points (R2>0.99) in a batch bioreactor (modified PETC-MES + 1.5 g/L yeast extract, continuous CO2/H2 gas flow 8 mL/min, pH 5.5 maintained with NaOH, 37°C, anaerobic). Culture is a soil-derived enrichment (mixed Caproicibacter sp. and C. thailandense, not an axenic isolate) that showed fluctuating gas-consumption patterns; doubling_time_h = ln2/0.029 = 23.9 h, computed here from the reported mu. Volumetric gas consumption rates and product titers (e.g., 53 mM acetate at stationary phase) are only shown graphically (Fig. 4), no numeric values in main text, so uptake/secretion rates could not be extracted.
